headline: Standard Headphones Can Spy On You Via Jack Retasking
lede: New research exposes how common audio chipsets allow software to flip output ports into input microphones. Even with physical camera covers, your everyday headphones can be coerced into recording your conversations without a dedicated mic.

headline: Byte Magazine's 1975 Debut Issue Returns to the Public
lede: The Internet Archive has unearthed the foundational first issue of Byte, a cornerstone of the microcomputer revolution. This relic documents the early DIY spirit that defined the first generation of consumer hardware.

headline: The Hidden Complexity Of The Game Pause Button
lede: Pausing a modern game requires far more than stopping a clock; it involves freezing physics engines, state stacks, and UI layers simultaneously. Developers often rely on clever, hacky workarounds to manage this delicate state consistency.

headline: Optimizing Data Warehouse Queries With Skiptrees
lede: To bypass the bottlenecks of recursive tree scanning in BigQuery, developers have implemented a hierarchical 'skiptree' structure. By flattening complex relationships into fixed joins, they achieved significant performance gains within an existing data environment.

headline: NIST Scientists Fabricate Laser Chips That Emit Any Color
lede: By stacking nonlinear materials onto silicon, researchers have miniaturized bulky laser systems into single, versatile photonic circuits. This advancement promises to bring laboratory-grade precision to portable quantum computing and communication tools.

headline: Anthropic Opus 4.7 Shows Spike In Token Consumption
lede: Community tracking reveals that the latest iteration of the Opus model consumes significantly more data to perform identical tasks. This increased overhead is forcing developers to reconsider their infrastructure costs and prompt strategies.

headline: Colleges Reclaim The Classroom With Manual Typewriters
lede: To foster genuine student composition in an age of automated text, some instructors are reverting to analog writing tools. Removing the screen removes the temptation, forcing students to slow down and think through their arguments.

headline: Celestial Navigation Inside The B-52 Bomber's Angle Computer
lede: Before the ubiquity of GPS, pilots relied on complex electromechanical assemblies to track stars in real-time. This specialized hardware solved spherical trigonometry using a system of gears and synchros that remains a masterclass in analog engineering.

headline: Identifying Phantom Binary Dependencies In Open Source
lede: Precompiled binaries often reside outside of standard manifest files, creating invisible security vulnerabilities. We must develop better tooling to map these hidden links and secure the foundation of our shared software infrastructure.

headline: How Legacy Standards Stalled The IPv6 Vision
lede: The original goal for IPv6 was to simplify the internet by removing archaic layer-2 dependencies. Instead, it became another layer in a stack of workarounds, proving that in networking, the old protocols never truly vanish.
pullquote: The inability to prune technical debt results in increasingly complicated workarounds that undermine the original, elegant simplicity proposed for IPv6.
